result:
**Assessing file import scope**I’m implementing EPAM-025 within the requested file scope first. I’ll inspect the four target files to understand the existing config and CLI patterns before editing.I have the target files loaded. Next I’m reading only the direct imports those files depend on where needed, so I can add the model-registry behavior without inventing incompatible shapes.**Clarifying test scope and orchestration metadata****Designing epam provider config****Clarifying model config and test scope**I have enough context to make the change. I’m going to add a small registry layer in config resolution, switch `epam models` from hardcoded catalog output to project-config output, and thread validated model metadata through `phase run` into the orchestration environment.**Planning substantial update****Designing model selection types****Designing deterministic model resolution****Planning config and model filtering**I’m editing the config layer first. The main change is to make EPAM model selection explicit in resolved config so the command layer can validate and log without duplicating logic.The type surface is in place. Next I’m moving the selection rules into `ConfigResolver` so `models` and `phase run` both consume the same resolved registry state.**Planning model command patch**I’m replacing the static model catalog now. `epam models` will read the resolved EPAM registry and surface the configured default instead of hardcoded provider data.**Adding new file****Planning phase.ts patch**I’m updating `phase run` next. The command will accept `--model`, validate it through the same resolver, log the resolved default path when no override is supplied, and inject the selection metadata into orchestration via env vars.**Confirming orchestration context use**The core behavior is in. I’m checking the edited files for type or logic issues before I try a build-oriented verification.**Removing unused imports and planning type-check**I’ve finished the code changes. I’m running a TypeScript compile check now; after that I’ll decide whether the test acceptance can be satisfied without breaking the user’s scope restriction.**Planning polling implementation**The compile is still running. If it fails, I’ll keep the fixes inside the same four files and avoid widening scope.**Deciding against adding tests due to scope**The compile check passed. I’m doing one small cleanup in `ConfigResolver` to keep the type imports aligned with what the file actually uses.**Planning quick type-check****Noting config cache gotcha****Summarizing final changes and limitations**Implemented EPAM model registry and selection across the requested files.

`[src/config/types.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/config/types.ts)` now supports `allowedModels`, `defaultModel`, and resolved model-selection metadata. `[src/config/ConfigResolver.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/config/ConfigResolver.ts)` now resolves EPAM models from the configured allow-list, validates explicit overrides, deterministically falls back to `defaultModel` or the first allowed model, and fixes config caching so flag-based model selection is not stale.

`[src/cli/commands/models.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/models.ts)` now lists the configured EPAM registry instead of a hardcoded catalog and marks the default. `[src/cli/commands/phase.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/phase.ts)` now accepts `--model`, validates it through config resolution, logs default-model resolution when no override is provided, includes model metadata in emitted phase events, and exports the selected model into orchestration context via env vars (`EPAM_MODEL`, `EPAM_DEFAULT_MODEL`, `EPAM_MODEL_SOURCE`, `EPAM_ALLOWED_MODELS`, etc.).

Verification: `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

Unit test files were not added because your instructions restricted scope to these four source files, which conflicts with creating or modifying separate test files. If you want, I can do a follow-up scoped to the relevant test files only.
